global.name: Anay Gawate
global.headline: CSE Student | Building apps that should exist | Flutter · Embedded · Self-taught since 14

homePage.tagline: I build things that should exist — apps, systems, and occasionally RC planes. Most of what I know came from just doing it.
homePage.linkedinTagline: CSE Student | Building apps that should exist | Flutter · Embedded · Self-taught since 14
homePage.bio: I've been building things on the internet since I was 14 — starting with Android apps in Java/Kotlin, crashing RC planes, and figuring out how computers actually work. Currently a CS student working across mobile, web, and embedded systems. Open to technical internships where I can actually build something.
aboutExtended: I've been building things on the internet since I was 14 — starting with Android apps in Java/Kotlin, crashing RC planes, and figuring out how computers actually work.

Currently a CS student, but most of what I know came from just doing things. I built a full cross-platform nutrition tracker in Flutter because the good ones cost money and I didn't think they should.
It runs on Android, iOS and web, uses Firebase for auth and database, and has a crowdsourced food database I've been building myself.
I work across the stack — Flutter, C/C++, Python, Java, web frontend, embedded systems, Linux, local LLMs.
I've deployed on Vercel, Netlify and Cloudflare, run models locally with Ollama and LM Studio, and I know my way around hardware as well as software.
Open to technical internships where I can actually build something.

experience.title: Independent Developer
experience.description: Independently developed and published Android applications from age 15, prior to AI-assisted development tools. First app reached 10K+ organic downloads on the Google Play Store. Continued shipping across Android, iOS, and Web, including a Flutter-based nutrition and health tracker backed by Firebase.

education.degree: Bachelor of Technology - BTech, Computer Science
education.description: Currently pursuing undergraduate studies in Computer Science while building cross-platform apps and systems projects.

projectsPage.title: My Projects
projectsPage.subtitle: A collection of things I've built — some polished, some still in progress, all real.

allProjects.title: PureFuel
allProjects.description: A cross-platform nutrition and health tracker for Android, iOS, and Web — built as a free, ad-free alternative to paid apps like Cronometer and HealthifyMe. Includes Firebase auth and database, and a crowdsourced food database. Long-term goal: open-source, donation-based.

allProjects.title: Journease - Travel Planner
allProjects.description: A smart travel companion for logging trips, memories, and getting AI-powered insights. Built for a university hackathon — placed in the top 100 teams out of 900+ in Smart India Hackathon.

allProjects.title: School Management System
allProjects.description: Building a full-featured cross-platform school management application in Flutter for a private school. Covers attendance, fee management, staff and student records, and parent communication.

allProjects.title: Lie Detector Prank App
allProjects.description: My first published Android app — built at 15, entirely self-taught, pre-AI. A prank lie detector app that crossed 10,000+ organic downloads on the Play Store and is still live. Not sophisticated, but it was real: coded, signed, published, and found by users on its own.

allProjects.title: Fixed-Wing RC Aircraft
allProjects.description: Designed and assembled a fixed-wing RC aircraft from scratch — FlySky TX/RX system, 2200KV brushless motor, ESC, LiPo battery, self-fabricated cardboard airframe. It flew. Then it didn't. Electronics are stored safely; the frame is the only casualty.

skillsPage.title: My Toolkit
skillsPage.subtitle: Languages, frameworks, and tools I actually use — not a buzzword list.

volunteering.organization: School of Computing, MIT ADT University
volunteering.description: Primary point of contact between 70+ students and faculty for 2 semesters in FY.

honorsAndAwards.title: Smart India Hackathon - Top 100 Teams
honorsAndAwards.description: Journease, a smart travel companion for logging trips, memories, analysis, and insights, was recognized among the top 100 teams out of 900+.
